architecture.summary: Sutra is a typed, purely functional programming language that compiles to a fused PyTorch tensor-operation graph over frozen embedding substrates. The compiler beta-reduces primitives, control flow, and string I/O to tensor operations. Rotation binding, unbinding, bundling, polynomial Kleene three-valued logic, and tail-recursive loops lower to tensor operations via Lagrange-interpolated polynomials. Information flows from symbolic source code through compilation to a single differentiable tensor graph that can run on multiple frozen embedding modalities (text encoders or protein language models) and backpropagate gradients through the compiled operations.

biblio.title: Sutra: Tensor-Op RNNs as a Compilation Target for Vector Symbolic Architectures

claims.dataset: four frozen embeddings (nomic-embed-text, all-minilm, mxbai-embed-large, ESM-2)
claims.statement: decodes bundles at 100% accuracy through width k=8 on every substrate, where the textbook Hadamard product has already collapsed
a fuzzy-rule classifier written in .su trains from random init to 100.0 +/- 0.0% by backpropagating through the emitted graph

claims.dataset: trained fuzzy-rule classifier with cosine gain
claims.statement: a weighted variant additionally trains a scalar cosine gain and writes it back into the .su source as a numeric literal; recompiling reproduces the trained behaviour to ~2e-7 per logit
The same program runs on four frozen embeddings spanning two modalities and decodes bundles at 100% accuracy through width k=8 on every substrate
PyTorch autograd flows through the actually compiled graph: the symbolic source unmodified trains from random init to 100% by backpropagating through the emitted graph
The same artifact is therefore both a logic program and a trainable neural network
